top_signals.title: Oil Extends Drop as More Tankers Cross Hormuz After Peace Talks
top_signals.summary: Oil extended declines as more tankers openly cross the Strait of Hormuz, while the US and Iran signal progress toward ending the war.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Iran Rushes to Woo Asia’s Top Oil Importers After US Waiver - Bloomberg
top_signals.summary: Iran Rushes to Woo Asia’s Top Oil Importers After US Waiver  Bloomberg
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: China detains two Japanese over alleged export control breach
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Stocks Waver as Traders Look Ahead to Micron: Markets Wrap
top_signals.summary: A measure of calm returned to equity markets after a selloff in top-performing technology names, with Micron Technology Inc.’s earnings taking on added significance as a test for the artificial intelligence trade.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Tankmaker KNDS lays out IPO plans amid defense selloff in Europe
top_signals.summary: KNDS plans to list shares in Paris and Frankfurt, with current shareholders expected to sell up to 20% of existing share capital.

top_signals.title: Tank maker KNDS plans stock market listing, Germany to buy 40% stake
top_signals.summary: The firm showed a proposed battle tank at Eurosatory defense exhibition in Paris boasting a French turret and gun mounted on a German Leopard 2 chassis.

top_signals.title: U.S. International Trade in Goods and Services, April 2026
top_signals.summary: The U.S. monthly international trade deficit decreased in April 2026 according to the U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis and the U.S. Census Bureau. The deficit decreased from $56.6 billion in March (revised) to $55.9 billion in April, as exports increased more than imports. The goods deficit decreased $2.4 billion in April to $83.7 billion. The services surplus decreased $1.7 billion in April to $27.8 billion. Full Text
